name: Steamed Salmon Fillets
recipeIngredient: 20 ounces salmon fillets, skin removed, cut into portions
recipeInstructions:
1. Fill the bottom of a steamer about half full with water and white wine vinegar.
2. Place the fish fillets on the rack of the steamer.
3. Sprinkle each fillet sparingly with salt, then add a sprinkle of coriander.
4. Add one sprig of rosemary or dill to each fillet.
5. Cover, bring to a boil, and steam for 5-6 minutes, or until the fish is tender when pierced with the tip of a knife and has lost its raw appearance.
6. To plate: Place the fish fillets on warm serving plates. Spoon warm Fennel Yogurt Sauce over and around them. Arrange cucumber slices and potato wedges in spokes radiating from the fish at the center.
Pat's variation: Grill without oil on a ridged griddle; omit vinegar; serve with lemon wedges.
Menu for Four: Salmon (7), Fennel and Yogurt Sauce (20), Steamed Parsleyed Potatoes with Cucumbers (15). 357.6 CALS, 6.1 G FAT (15.2% CFF), 422 MG SODIUM. Based on the PBS series: Pierre Franey's Cooking in Europe. Tested by Pat H. June 1997.
